- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

1	# Behavioral spec inferred from the oracle (wellformedness checker)
2	
3	All facts below are derived from oracle observation (probes in
4	scratchpad/probes, captures in oracle/captures). Nothing here is taken from
5	tamarin-prover source.
6	
7	## Overall report shape
8	
9	The oracle emits the wellformedness result inside the pretty-printed theory,
10	wrapped in a block comment.
11	
12	Success (empty report):
13	
14	    /* All wellformedness checks were successful. */
15	
16	Failure (>=1 topic):
17	
18	    /*
19	    WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!
20	
21	    <block1>
22	
23	    <block2>
24	    ...
25	    */
26	
27	Inner text (what `render_report` produces for a non-empty report) is:
28	
29	    "WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!" + "\n\n" + join(blocks, "\n\n")
30	
31	Each `block` = topic header + "\n" + underline + "\n\n" + body, where
32	`underline` = '=' repeated char_count(topic) (topic length INCLUDING any
33	leading/trailing space). Bodies may themselves contain blank lines.
34	
35	`render_report` for the empty report returns the success line
36	`/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */`.
37	`underline_topic(t)` returns `t + "\n" + "=".repeat(char_count(t))`.
38	
39	## Canonical topic order (report order)
40	
41	Observed by kitchen-sink probes ks1 / ks3 and the round-2 ordering probes
42	(comb1, ord_tail, mega_tail, diff_mega, diff_place):
43	
44	 1. `Unbound variables`
45	 2. `Fresh public constants`                             (round2; always-on)
46	 3. `Public constants with mismatching capitalization`   (public-names report)
47	 4. `Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization`
48	 5. `Reserved names`
49	 5a. `Reserved prefixes`                                 (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
50	 6. `Fr facts must only use a fresh- or a msg-variable`
51	 7. `Special facts`
52	 7b. `Fact capitalization issues`                        (round3; before arity)
53	 8. `Fact arity issues`                                  (non-diff; diff merges these
54	 9. `Fact multiplicity issues`                            two into "Fact usage" - OUT OF SCOPE)
55	10. `Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side ` (TRAILING space)
56	10a. `Left rule` / `Right rule`                          (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
57	11. `Formula terms`
58	12. ` Formula guardedness`   (LEADING space)
59	13. `Lemma annotations`                                  (round2; always-on)
60	14. `Multiplication restriction of rules`                (round2; always-on)
61	15. `Nat Sorts`
62	16. `Subterm Convergence Warning`
63	17. `Message Derivation Checks` (appended by binary; needs Maude - OUT OF SCOPE)
64	    / `Derivation Checks` (timeout variant - OUT OF SCOPE)
65	
66	Round-2 ordering evidence: comb1 -> `Unbound, Fresh public constants,
67	Public names, mismatching sorts, Reserved`; mega_tail -> `Formula terms,
68	Formula guardedness, Lemma annotations, Multiplication restriction, Nat Sorts,
69	Subterm`; diff_mega/diff_place -> `... Reserved names, Reserved prefixes,
70	Fr facts, ... lhs-not-rhs, Left/Right rule, Formula terms, ...`.
71	
72	Public names (#3) is generated separately and *inserted before* the first of a
73	set of anchor topics = every topic from #4 (mismatching sorts) onward.
74	`after_public_names_topics()` returns that anchor list; `insert_wf_before(
75	report, errs, anchors)` inserts `errs` immediately before the first report
76	entry whose topic is in `anchors` (appends at end if none present). Unbound
77	variables (#1) and Fresh public constants (#2) are emitted before the
78	public-names insertion point (so they are NOT anchors).
79	
80	### Diff-mode-only vs. always-on
81	
82	Gated on `theory.is_diff`: `Reserved prefixes`, `Left rule`, `Right rule`
83	(observed silent in non-diff mode; diff_reserved_prefix without `--diff` emits
84	nothing). Always-on (both modes): `Fresh public constants`, `Lemma
85	annotations`, `Multiplication restriction of rules`.
86	
87	## Per-check triggers and message templates
88	
89	### Unbound variables
90	Trigger: a protocol rule uses a variable in actions/conclusions not bound by a
91	premise. Entries joined by "\n  \n":
92	    "  rule `NAME' has unbound variables: \n    v1, v2"
93	(trailing space after "variables:"). Vars sorted, ", " joined, term-printed.
94	
95	### Public constants with mismatching capitalization (public_names_report)
96	Trigger: two public constants whose names are equal under ASCII-lowercasing but
97	differ in capitalization. Body:
98	    "Identifiers are case-sensitive, mismatched capitalizations are considered as different, i.e., 'ID' is different from 'id'. Check the capitalization of your identifiers.\n\n" + numbered items
99	Each lowercased-key group -> one item "  N. " + occurrences. Occurrences group by
100	location; same loc: `rule "R1":  name 'Alice', 'alice'`; different locs joined
101	", ": `rule "R1":  name 'Server', rule "R2":  name 'server'`. Names ASCII-sorted.
102	`public_names_report_from_pairs` takes raw (location, name) pairs.
103	
104	### Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization
105	Trigger: within one rule, two variables share a base name but differ in sort
106	prefix ($x/x/~x) or capitalization. Fixed header:
107	    "Possible reasons:\n1. Identifiers are case sensitive, i.e.,'x' and 'X' are considered to be different.\n2. The same holds for sorts:, i.e., '$x', 'x', and '~x' are considered to be different.\n"
108	per rule: "  rule `A5': \n    1. $s, ~s" (trailing space; numbered variant groups).
109	
110	### Reserved names
111	Trigger: rule uses a fact with a reserved name in a position. The reserved set
112	is POSITION-DEPENDENT (observed z9/z11/z12):
113	  - left-hand-side / right-hand-side: {K, KU, KD}
114	  - the middle (actions): {K, KU, KD, In, Out, Fr}  (the I/O facts are also
115	    reserved as actions; on LHS/RHS they are "Special facts" instead)
116	Per (rule, position) entry joined "\n  \n":
117	    "  Rule `R1' contains facts with reserved names on left-hand-side:\n    <facts>"
118	Position phrases: LHS `on left-hand-side:`, actions `on the middle:`,
119	RHS `on the right-hand-side:`. Facts via fact printer, fsep comma-joined.
120	
121	### Fr facts must only use a fresh- or a msg-variable
122	Trigger: `Fr(t)` where t is not fresh/msg var. Entries joined "\n  \n":
123	    "  rule `R1' fact: Fr( $p )"
124	
125	### Special facts
126	Trigger: reserved I/O fact in disallowed position (Out/Fr/K in premises,
127	In/K in conclusions). Per (rule, side) joined "\n  \n":
128	    "  rule `R1' uses disallowed facts on left-hand-side:\n    Out( x )"
129	(sides `on left-hand-side:`/`on right-hand-side:`; lowercase "rule").
130	
131	### Fact arity issues
132	Trigger: same fact name with >1 arity. Body:
133	    "Same fact is used with different arities, i.e., Fact('A','B') is different from Fact('A'). \nCheck the arguments of your facts.\n  " + factBlocks
134	facts sorted by lowercased name; factBlock (each prefixed "\n"):
135	    "\n  Fact `foo':\n\n" + items + "\n  "
136	item i: "    {i}. {Label} `{owner}', arity {n}\n         {render}"; items joined
137	"\n    \n". Rules and LEMMAS both contribute (interleaved in theory-item order);
138	restrictions do NOT (r3_restrarity). Rule facts: Label=`Rule`, render=fact pp.
139	Lemma action facts: Label=`Lemma`, render = raw Haskell `Fact {factTag =
140	ProtoFact {Linear|Persistent} "{name}" {arity}, factAnnotations = fromList [],
141	factTerms = [{de-Bruijn terms}]}` (r3_lemarity). Rule items dedup per
142	(label,owner,arity); lemma facts likewise. Multiplicity is analogous and ALSO
143	gathers lemma facts (r3_lemmult, item = `... multiplicity (persistence) ...`).
144	
145	### Fact multiplicity issues
146	Same structure keyed on multiplicity; header:
147	    "Same fact is used with different multiplicities, i.e., !Fact() (Persistent fact) exists along with Fact() (Linear) in your rules. \nCheck the multiplicity (persistence) of your facts.\n  " + factBlocks
148	item i: "    {i}. {Label} `{owner}', multiplicity (persistence) {Linear|Persistent}\n         {render}".
149	
150	### Fact capitalization issues (round3; precedes Fact arity issues)
151	Trigger: two facts whose names are equal under ASCII-lowercasing but differ in
152	exact spelling (e.g. `Send` vs `SEND`; facts must start upper-case, so the
153	difference is in non-first letters). Same block shape as Fact arity, header:
154	    "Fact names are case-sensitive, different capitalizations are considered as different facts, i.e., Fact() is different from FAct(). \nCheck the capitalization of your fact names.\n  " + factBlocks
155	Unlike arity, EVERY occurrence is listed (NO per-(rule,cap) dedup - `Send` twice
156	in one rule -> two items; r3_capord). item i:
157	    "    {i}. Rule `{rule}', capitalization \"{exactName}\"\n         {factPP}".
158	Groups keyed by lowercased name, sorted; a group needs >=2 distinct spellings.
159	Gathers rule facts across premises/actions/conclusions (source order).
160	
161	### Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side  (fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs)
162	Trigger: user fact identity (name,arity,mult) in some premise, never in any
163	conclusion. Numbered items joined "\n  \n":
164	    "  1. in rule \"r1\":  factName `X' arity: 0 multiplicity: Linear" +
165	      optional ". Perhaps you want to use the fact in rule \"r1\":  factName `Y' arity: 0 multiplicity: Linear"
166	suggestion = nearest RHS fact by edit distance under a threshold (omitted when
167	nothing close). Special facts excluded from both sides.
168	
169	### Fresh public constants (round2; always-on)
170	Trigger: a fresh-name literal (`~'foo'`, AST `Term::FreshLit`) used directly in
171	a rule. Constants collected in the order premises, conclusions, actions
172	(probe fpc_positions). Per rule with >=1 hit, entries joined "\n  \n":
173	    "  rule `NAME': fresh public constants are not allowed: ~'foo', ~'bar'"
174	constants rendered by the term printer. The list is a fillSep at width 69 with a
175	4-space continuation indent, begun after the header `  rule `{name}': fresh
176	public constants are not allowed:` (round3: r3_freshwrap; identical mechanism to
177	the Formula-terms list). CLOSED - was a documented gap.
178	
179	### Reserved prefixes (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
180	Trigger (diff theories only): a fact whose name starts with `DiffIntr` or
181	`DiffProto`. Facts collected in the order premises, actions, conclusions
182	(probe rp_multi). Per rule the body is:
183	    "  The Rule `NAME' contains facts with reserved prefixes ('DiffIntr',\n  'DiffProto') inside names:\n  \n" + factblocks
184	The HEADER is word-filled (Wadler `fillSep`) at WIDTH 69, indent 2 (measured via
185	rp_long/rp_med/rp_w47: a line breaks before the next word once column would
186	exceed 69). Words treat the rule name `\`NAME'` and each of `('DiffIntr',` /
187	`'DiffProto')` as single tokens. Each factblock (joined "\n  \n"):
188	    "    <fact_pp>\n    (ProtoFact <Mult> \"<name>\" <arity>,<arity>,<Mult>)"
189	The 2nd line is a raw Haskell Show of a (FactTag, arity, Multiplicity) tuple;
190	<Mult> is `Linear`/`Persistent`. GAP: multi-RULE joining unprobed (fixtures use
191	one rule).
192	
193	### Left rule / Right rule (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
194	Trigger (diff theories only): a diff rule (`Rule.left_right = Some((l,r))`)
195	whose explicit `left`/`right` projection differs from the parent rule projected
196	to that side (`diff(a,b)` -> a on left, -> b on right). Per rule the LEFT
197	projection is tested first; if inconsistent the RIGHT is not reported for that
198	rule (probe diff_both: both sides bad -> only "Left rule"). Body per entry
199	(joined "\n  \n"):
200	    "  Inconsistent left rule\n" + indent4(explicit_rule_pp) +
201	    "\n  \n  w.r.t.\n  \n" + indent4(parent_rule_pp)
202	("left"/"right" and topic "Left rule"/"Right rule"). Consistency compared via
203	the projected vs. explicit fact lists. Left rule topic precedes Right rule.
204	
205	### Lemma annotations (round2; always-on)
206	Trigger: a lemma with attribute `reuse` AND quantifier `exists-trace` (reuse on
207	`all-traces` is fine - probe la_alltraces). Entries joined "\n  \n":
208	    "  Lemma `NAME': cannot reuse 'exists-trace' lemmas"
209	(Topic may host other annotation conflicts not yet observed.)
210	
211	### Multiplication restriction of rules (round2; always-on)
212	Trigger: a rule whose CONCLUSIONS contain a multiplication (`*`, AST
213	`BinOp::Mult`) term. `*` only in premises or only in actions does NOT trigger
214	(probes mul_multi, mul_act); a `*` in a premise is not listed even when the
215	conclusion also has one (mul_pc). Per rule, entry (joined "\n  \n"):
216	    "  The following rule is not multiplication restricted:\n" + indent4(rule_pp) +
217	    "\n  \n  After replacing reducible function symbols in lhs with variables:\n" + indent4(rule2_pp) +
218	    "\n  \n    Terms with multiplication:  <terms>"
219	<terms> = maximal `*`-subterms of the conclusions, ", " joined (mul_terms).
220	GAPS: (a) rule2 (LHS reducible symbols replaced by fresh vars) is rendered equal
221	to rule1 - correct only when the LHS has no reducible symbols (probe mul_exp
222	shows `g^c` -> `x.1` otherwise); (b) the alternate failure mode
223	"Variables that occur only in rhs:  <vars>" (mul_exp) is not implemented; (c)
224	the co-emitted `Message Derivation Checks` block is Maude-derived, OUT OF SCOPE;
225	(d) rule_pp wrapping for long rules (see printer note).
226	
227	### Formula terms  (round3: FULL coverage)
228	Trigger: a lemma/restriction formula uses a TERM "of the wrong form". The unit
229	checked is each ARGUMENT TERM of each atom (Eq/Less/.. -> both sides; Action ->
230	the TEMPORAL first, then the fact args; Pred/Last -> args). A term is wrong iff
231	it contains a FREE variable OR a REDUCIBLE function symbol; the WHOLE top-level
232	term is then reported (not the offending subterm). Body:
233	    "  {Entity} `{name}' uses terms of the wrong form: {termlist}\n  \n" + fixed_help
234	Entity = `Lemma` or `Restriction`. Terms are collected in source order and are
235	NOT deduplicated (`x=y & x=y` -> `Free y', `Free y'`). Reducibility is decided by
236	the CALLER, which supplies a set of reducible function-symbol names; the checker
237	only consumes it (entry point `formula_terms_reducible(thy, &reducible)`;
238	`formula_terms(thy)` = the empty-set convenience wrapper - free variables only).
239	
240	Raw term rendering (each term wrapped in `` `...' ``):
241	  - bound variable   -> `Bound N`   (de Bruijn: 0 = innermost binder; EVERY
242	                                      quantified var, incl. temporals, pushes one)
243	  - free variable    -> `Free <pp_var>`  (keeps the sort prefix, e.g. `Free #j`)
244	  - function app      -> `f(a,b)`   (args comma-joined, NO space)
245	  - tuple             -> `pair(a,pair(b,c))`  (right-nested binary pairs)
246	  - public constant   -> `'name'`
247	De Bruijn evidence: `All x y #i. h(x,y)` -> `h(Bound 2,Bound 1)`; nesting adds
248	depth (`All x #i.(Ex y #j. h(x))` -> `h(Bound 3)`). Free-inside-term evidence:
249	`f(y)` (f non-reducible, y free) -> `f(Free y)`; `f(h(x))` -> `f(h(Bound 1))`.
250	
251	Term-list layout is a fillSep at width 69 with a 4-space continuation indent,
252	begun after the header (`  {Entity} `{name}' uses terms of the wrong form:`): a
253	token (term + trailing comma, except the last) stays on the line while
254	`col + 1 + width <= 69`, else wraps. A long entity/name can push the entire list
255	to line 2; an over-wide single term also wraps to line 2. Multiple wrong lemmas:
256	each full block (header+list+help) is joined by `\n  \n`.
257	fixed_help = "  The only allowed terms are public constants and bound node and\n  message variables. If you encounter free message variables, then\n  you might have forgotten a #-prefix. Sort prefixes can only be\n  dropped where this is unambiguous. Moreover, reducible function\n  symbols are disallowed."

259	### Formula guardedness (topic has LEADING space)  (round3: two modes + wrapping)
260	Trigger: a LEMMA formula not convertible to guarded form. (An unguarded
261	RESTRICTION is instead a FATAL error, not a warning -- observed z1; lemmas only.)
262	Body:
263	    "  Lemma `{name}' cannot be converted to a guarded formula:\n    {reason}\n      \"<sub>\"\n    in the formula\n      \"<whole>\""
264	where <sub> is the FAILING QUANTIFIER SUBTREE (not the whole formula) and <whole>
265	is the full lemma formula. Two observed reasons (probe r3_gc):
266	  - `unguarded variable(s) '#j', ... in the subformula`  (quantifier binds vars
267	    not guarded by an action fact)
268	  - `universal quantifier without toplevel implication`
269	Guardedness decision (∀): the body must be a top-level `guard ==> rest`; its
270	ANTECEDENT's action facts (temporal incl.) must bind every quantified var
271	(consequent does NOT guard). A conjunction/disjunction/negation/atom body -> "no
272	toplevel implication". A guarded ∀ recurses into antecedent and consequent
273	(finds nested failures; the reported subformula is the inner failing quantifier).
274	∃ takes a conjunctive guard (`Ex x #i. A(x)@#i` is fine); an ∃ with unguarded
275	vars is "unguarded variable(s)".
276	
277	<sub>/<whole> use the multi-line formula printer (pp_formula_wrapped): a small
278	HughesPJ-style engine at page width ~72 (single line fits at total col 72, breaks
279	by 74). Rules (calibrated r3_gw/r3_qm/r3_and):
280	  - atom -> single-line text (never breaks); connective operands always parens'd.
281	  - binary op `(a) OP (b)`: breaks after OP; the right operand hangs at the
282	    connective's START COLUMN (the column just after its enclosing `(`).
283	  - quantifier `Q vars. body`: breaks after the `.`; body hangs at base+2
284	    (base = the quote indent 6, so body at col 8).
285	The formula is embedded as `      "..."` (quote col 6, formula col 7). When a
286	formula fits on one line the printer is byte-identical to the single-line
287	`pp_formula`, so narrow-formula fixtures are unaffected.
288

291	    "  y in term (y%+z) must be of sort nat" (nat-sort inference - structural only, gap).

294	Trigger: user equation l=r not subterm convergent. Body:
295	    "  User-defined equations must be convergent and have the finite variant property. The following equations are not subterm convergent. If you are sure that the set of equations is nevertheless convergent and has the finite variant property, you can ignore this warning and continue \n\n" + eqs + "\n   \n For more information, please refer to the manual : https://tamarin-prover.com/manual/master/book/010_modeling-issues.html "
296	each eq "    {lhsPP} = {rhsPP}" (subterm decision + printer partial - gap).

299	- Var: sortprefix + base + ("."+idx if idx>0). Fresh `~`, Pub `$`, Nat `%`,
300	  Node `#`, Msg/Untagged ``.
301	- Pair / App("pair",[a,b]): `<a, b, ...>` (right-nested pairs flattened).
302	- App(f,args): `f(a, b)` (nullary -> `f`).
303	- PubLit s: `'s'`.
304	- BinOp: Exp `a^b` (no outer parens); Mult `(a*b)`; Union `(a++b)`;
305	  Xor `(a(+)b)` U+2295 AC-sorted; NatPlus `(a%+b)`.
306	- NatOne -> `%1`.
307	- Fact: [`!` if persistent] + name + "(" + (" "+args.join(", ")+" )" if args else " )").

310	Used by Left/Right rule and Multiplication restriction. Base form (single line

312	    "rule (modulo E) NAME:\n   [ prems ] <arrow> [ concls ]"
313	- 3-space indent before the fact-list line; fact lists `[ f1, f2 ]`, `[ ]` empty.
314	- <arrow> = `-->` (no actions) or `--[ acts ]->` (actions present).

322	For free-variable detection (Formula terms) and guardedness the oracle treats a
323	quantified message variable and its occurrences as ONE variable even when the
324	parsed AST tags them with different-but-compatible sorts (Msg vs the Untagged

182	mostSimilarName lhs rhs =

184	  where

353	--- | Check that the protocol rule variants are correct.
354	variantsCheck :: MaudeHandle -> [LNMacro] -> String -> OpenProtoRule -> WfErrorReport
355	variantsCheck hnd macros info (OpenProtoRule ruE ruAC) = catMaybes
356	  [ guard (not (null ruAC) && not (sameVariantsUpToActions ruAC recomputedVariants)) $>
357	      ( underlineTopic "Variants"
358	      , text info $-$ nest 2 (numbered' (map prettyProtoRuleAC ruAC))
359	        $--$ text "Recomputed variants: " $--$
360	        nest 2 (numbered' $ map prettyProtoRuleAC recomputedVariants)
361	      )
362	  , guard (null recomputedVariants) $>
363	      ( underlineTopic "Rule has no variants"
364	      ,       text "Rule " <> prettyRuleName ruE <> text " has no variants."
365	        $--$  text "Most likely, this means that the rule's use of fresh variables is contradictory. "
366	        <>    text "For exaple, a rule with the premises In(~x) and Fr(~x) has no variants because ~x cannot be sent before it is generated." )]
367	  where
368	    recomputedVariants =
369	      map (get cprRuleAC) $
370	      concatMap (unfoldRuleVariants . ClosedProtoRule ruE) $
371	      maybeToList (variantsProtoRule hnd (applyMacroInRule macros ruE))
372	    sameVariantsUpToActions parsed computed = all (\x -> any (equalUpToAddedActions x) computed) parsed

492	-- | Check whether a rule has unbound variables.
493	unboundCheck :: Document b => String -> Rule ProtoRuleEInfo -> [([Char], b)]
494	unboundCheck info ru
495	    | null unboundVars = []
496	    | otherwise        = return $
497	        ( underlineTopic "Unbound variables"
498	        , text info $-$ nest 2 (prettyVarList unboundVars) )
499	  where
500	    boundVars   = S.fromList $ frees (get rPrems ru)
501	    originatesFromLookup v = match v $ ruleProcess $ get preAttributes $ get rInfo ru
502	    match v (Just (ProcessComb (Lookup _ v') _ _ _))  = v == slvar v'
503	    match _ _ = False
504	    isNowNode v = lvarSort v == LSortNode && lvarName v == "NOW"
505	    unboundVars = do
506	        v <- frees (get rConcs ru, get rActs ru, get rInfo ru)
507	        guard $ not ( isNowNode v
508	                    || lvarSort v == LSortPub
509	                    || v `S.member` boundVars
510	                    || originatesFromLookup v)
511	        return v

535	                  || isKLogFact fa]
536	      mfact = [fa | fa <- get rActs ru
537	                  , factTag fa `elem` [KUFact,KDFact,InFact,OutFact,FreshFact]
538	                  || isKLogFact fa]
539	      rfact = [fa | fa <- get rConcs ru

541	                  || isKLogFact fa]

578	-- | Report on facts usage. Skip checks on non-existant actions if `incompleteMSRs` is True.
579	factReports :: Bool -> OpenTranslatedTheory -> WfErrorReport
580	factReports incompleteMSRs thy =
581	    concat  [ reservedReport, reservedFactNameRules, freshFactArguments, specialFactsUsage
582	    , factUsage, factLhsOccurNoRhs]
583	    ++ concat [ inexistentActions ++ inexistentActionsRestrictions | incompleteMSRs ]
584	  where
585	    ruleFacts ru =
586	      ( "Rule " ++ quote (showRuleCaseName ru)
587	      , extFactInfo <$> concatMap (`get` ru) [rPrems, rActs, rConcs])
588	
589	    -- NOTE: The check that the number of actual function arguments in a term
590	    -- agrees with the arity of the function as given by the signature is
591	    -- enforced by the parser and implicitly checked in 'factArity'.
592	
593	    theoryFacts = -- sortednubOn (fst &&& (snd . snd)) $
594	          do ruleFacts <$> get thyCache thy
595	      <|> ((do
596	             ru <- thyProtoRules thy
597	             return $ ruleFacts $ ru)
598	          ++ (do
599	             RuleItem ru <- get thyItems thy
600	             ruAC <- get oprRuleAC ru
601	             return $ ruleFacts ruAC))
602	      <|> do LemmaItem l <- get thyItems thy
603	             return $ (,) ("Lemma " ++ quote (get lName l)) $ do
604	                 fa <- formulaFacts (get lFormula l)
605	                 return $ (text (show fa), factInfo fa)
606	
607	    -- we must compute all important information up-front in order to
608	    -- mangle facts with terms with bound variables and such without them
609	    extFactInfo fa = (prettyLNFact fa, factInfo fa)
610	
611	    --- Check for usage of protocol facts with reserved names
612	    reservedReport = do
613	        (origin, fas) <- theoryFacts
614	        case mapMaybe reservedFactName fas of
615	          []   -> []
616	          errs -> return $ (,) (underlineTopic "Reserved names") $ foldr1 ($--$) $
617	              wrappedText ("The " ++ origin ++
618	                           " contains facts with reserved names:")
619	            : map (nest 2) errs
620	
621	    reservedFactName (ppFa, info@(ProtoFact _ name _, _,_))
622	      | map toLower name `elem` ["fr","ku","kd","out","in"] =
623	          return $ ppFa $-$ text ("show:" ++ show info)
624	    reservedFactName _ = Nothing
625	
626	    -- Check for usage of all type facts with reserved names
627	    reservedFactNameRules = reservedFactNameRules' $ thyProtoRules thy
628	
629	    freshFactArguments = freshFactArguments' $ thyProtoRules thy
630	
631	    -- Check for the usage of special facts at wrong positions
632	    specialFactsUsage = specialFactsUsage' $ thyProtoRules thy
633	
634	    -- Check for facts with equal name modulo capitalization, but different
635	    -- multiplicity or arity.
636	    factUsage :: WfErrorReport
637	    factUsage = capIssues ++ arityIssues ++ multipIssues
638	      where
639	        theoryFacts' = [(ru, fa) | (ru, fas) <- theoryFacts, fa <- fas]
640	        factIdentifier (_, (_, (tag, _, _))) = map toLower $ factTagName tag
641	        allClashes = filter (\g -> length g > 1) $ 
642	                    groupOn factIdentifier $ 
643	                    sortOn factIdentifier theoryFacts'
644	        capIssues = 
645	          if any hasCapIssue allClashes then
646	            [(underlineTopic "Fact capitalization issues" ++ "\n" ++ capIssueMsg, 
647	              text "\n" $-$ vcat (map formatCapIssue $ filter hasCapIssue allClashes))]
648	          else []
649	        arityIssues = 
650	          if any hasArityIssue allClashes then
651	            [(underlineTopic "Fact arity issues" ++ "\n" ++ arityIssueMsg,
652	              text "\n" $-$ vcat (map formatArityIssue $ filter hasArityIssue allClashes))]
653	          else []
654	        multipIssues = 
655	          if any hasMultipIssue allClashes then
656	            [(underlineTopic "Fact multiplicity issues" ++ "\n" ++ multipIssueMsg,
657	              text "\n" $-$ vcat (map formatMultipIssue $ filter hasMultipIssue allClashes))]
658	          else []
659	        
660	        formatCapIssue clash = 
661	          text ("Fact `" ++ name clash ++ "':\n") $-$
662	          nest 2 (numbered' $ 
663	            [ text (origin ++ ", capitalization " ++ show (factTagName tag)) $-$ nest 2 ppFa 
664	            | (origin, (ppFa, (tag, _, _))) <- clash ])$-$ text ""
665	        formatArityIssue clash = 
666	          text ("Fact `" ++ name clash ++ "':\n") $-$
667	          nest 2 (numbered' $ 
668	            [ text (origin ++ ", arity " ++ show arity) $-$ nest 2 ppFa 
669	            | (origin, (ppFa, (_, arity, _))) <- clash ]) $-$ text ""
670	        formatMultipIssue clash = 
671	          text ("Fact `" ++ name clash ++ "':\n") $-$
672	          nest 2 (numbered' $ 
673	            [ text (origin ++ ", multiplicity (persistence) " ++ show multip) $-$ nest 2 ppFa 
674	            | (origin, (ppFa, (_, _, multip))) <- clash ])$-$ text ""
675	
676	        hasCapIssue clash = length (sortednub [factTagName tag | (_, (_, (tag, _, _))) <- clash]) > 1
677	        hasArityIssue clash = length (sortednub [arity | (_, (_, (_, arity, _))) <- clash]) > 1
678	        hasMultipIssue clash = length (sortednub [multip | (_, (_, (_, _, multip))) <- clash]) > 1
679	        name clash = map toLower $ factTagName $ let (_, (_, (tag, _, _))) = head clash in tag
680	        capIssueMsg = "Fact names are case-sensitive, different capitalizations are " ++
681	                    "considered as different facts, " ++
682	                    "i.e., Fact() is different from FAct(). \n" ++
683	                    "Check the capitalization of your fact names."
684	        arityIssueMsg = "Same fact is used with different arities, " ++
685	                      "i.e., Fact('A','B') is different from Fact('A'). \n" ++
686	                      "Check the arguments of your facts."
687	        multipIssueMsg = "Same fact is used with different multiplicities, " ++
688	                        "i.e., !Fact() (Persistent fact) exists along with Fact() (Linear) in your rules. \n" ++
689	                        "Check the multiplicity (persistence) of your facts."
690	
691	
692	    -- Check that every fact referenced in a formula is present as an action
693	    -- of a protocol rule. We have to add the linear "K/1" fact, as the
694	    -- WF-check cannot rely on a loaded intruder theory.
695	    ruleActions = S.fromList $ map factInfo $
696	          kLogFact undefined
697	        : dedLogFact undefined
698	        : kuFact undefined
699	        : (do ru <- thyProtoRules thy; get rActs ru)
700	          ++ (do RuleItem ru <- get thyItems thy; racs <- get oprRuleAC ru; get rActs racs)
701	
702	    -- Report a protocol fact occurs in an LHS but not in any RHS
703	    factLhsOccurNoRhs :: WfErrorReport
704	    factLhsOccurNoRhs = factLhsOccurNoRhs' $ thyProtoRules thy
705	
706	    inexistentActions = do
707	        LemmaItem l <- get thyItems thy
708	        fa <- sortednub $ formulaFacts (get lFormula l)
709	        let info = factInfo fa
710	            name = get lName l
711	            (tag,ari,mul)=info
712	        if info `S.member` ruleActions
713	          then []
714	          else return $ (,) (underlineTopic "Inexistent lemma actions") $
715	                 text ("Lemma " ++ quote name ++ " references action ") $-$
716	                 nest 2 (text ("fact " ++ show (factTagName tag)++
717	                 " (arity "++ show ari++
718	                 ", "++show mul++") ")) $-$
719	                 text "but no rule has such an action."
720	
721	    inexistentActionsRestrictions = do
722	        RestrictionItem l <- get thyItems thy
723	        fa <- sortednub $ formulaFacts (get rstrFormula l)
724	        let info = factInfo fa
725	            name = get rstrName l
726	            (tag,ari,mul)=info
727	        if info `S.member` ruleActions
728	          then []
729	          else return $ (,) (underlineTopic "Inexistent restriction actions") $
730	                 text ("Restriction " ++ quote name ++ " references action ") $-$
731	                 nest 2 (text ("fact " ++ show (factTagName tag)++
732	                 " (arity "++ show ari++
733	                 ", "++show mul++") ")) $-$
734	                 text "but no rule has such an action."
735

803	            : map (nest 2) errs

810	    freshFactArguments = freshFactArguments' $ diffThyProtoRules thy
811	
812	    -- Check for the usage of special facts at wrong positions

570	-- Induction over the trace
571	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
572	
573	-- | Negate a guarded formula.
574	gnot :: (Ord s, Ord c, Ord v)
575	     => Guarded s c v -> Guarded s c v
576	gnot =
577	    go
578	  where
579	    go (GGuarded All ss as gf) = gex  ss as $ go gf
580	    go (GGuarded Ex ss as gf)  = gall ss as $ go gf
581	    go (GAto ato)              = gnotAtom ato
582	    go (GDisj disj)            = gconj $ map go (getDisj disj)
583	    go (GConj conj)            = gdisj $ map go (getConj conj)
584	
585	
586	-- | Checks if a doubly guarded formula is satisfied by the empty trace;
587	-- returns @'Left' errMsg@ if the formula is not doubly guarded.
588	satisfiedByEmptyTrace :: Guarded s c v -> Either String Bool
589	satisfiedByEmptyTrace =
590	  foldGuarded
591	    (\_ato -> throwError "atom outside the scope of a quantifier")
592	    (liftM or  . sequence . getDisj)
593	    (liftM and . sequence . getConj)
594	    (\qua _ss _as _gf -> return $ qua == All)
595	    -- the empty trace always satisfies guarded all-quantification
596	    -- and always dissatisfies guarded ex-quantification
597	
598	-- | Tries to convert a doubly guarded formula to an induction hypothesis.
599	-- Returns @'Left' errMsg@ if the formula is not last-free or not doubly
600	-- guarded.
601	toInductionHypothesis :: Ord c => LGuarded c -> Either String (LGuarded c)
602	toInductionHypothesis =
603	    go
604	  where
605	    go (GGuarded qua ss as gf)
606	      | any isLastAtom as = throwError "formula not last-free"
607	      | otherwise         = do
608	          gf' <- go gf
609	          return $ case qua of
610	            All -> gex  ss as (gconj $ (gnotAtom <$> lastAtos) ++ [gf'])
611	            Ex  -> gall ss as (gdisj $ (GAto <$> lastAtos) ++ [gf'])
612	      where
613	        lastAtos :: [Atom (VTerm c (BVar LVar))]
614	        lastAtos = do
615	            (j, (_, LSortNode)) <- zip [0..] $ reverse ss
616	            return $ Last (varTerm (Bound j))
617	
618	    go (GAto (Less i j)) = return $ gdisj [GAto (EqE i j), GAto (Less j i)]
619	    go (GAto (Last _))   = throwError "formula not last-free"
620	    go (GAto ato)        = return $ gnotAtom ato
621	    go (GDisj disj)      = gconj <$> traverse go (getDisj disj)
622	    go (GConj conj)      = gdisj <$> traverse go (getConj conj)
623	
624	-- | Try to prove the formula by applying induction over the trace.
625	-- Returns @'Left' errMsg@ if this is not possible. Returns a tuple of
626	-- formulas: one formalizing the proof obligation of the base-case and one
627	-- formalizing the proof obligation of the step-case.
628	ginduct :: Ord c => LGuarded c -> Either String (LGuarded c, LGuarded c)
629	ginduct gf = do
630	    unless (null $ frees gf)   (throwError "formula not closed")
631	    unless (containsAction gf) (throwError "formula contains no action atom")
632	    baseCase <- satisfiedByEmptyTrace gf
633	    gfIH     <- toInductionHypothesis gf
634	    return (gtf baseCase, gconj [gf, gfIH])
635	  where
636	    containsAction = foldGuarded (const True) (or . getDisj) (or . getConj)
637	                                 (\_ _ as body -> not (null as) || body)
638

641	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
642	
643	-- | Simplify a 'Guarded' formula by replacing atoms with their truth value,
644	-- if it can be determined.
645	simplifyGuarded :: (LNAtom -> Maybe Bool)
646	                -- ^ Partial assignment for truth value of atoms.
647	                -> LNGuarded
648	                -- ^ Original formula
649	                -> Bool
650	                -- ^ Verbose parameter
651	                -> Maybe LNGuarded
652	                -- ^ Simplified formula, provided some simplification was
653	                -- performed.
654	simplifyGuarded valuation fm0 verbose
655	    | fm1 /= fm0 = if (verbose) then trace (render $ ppMsg) (Just fm1) else (Just fm1)
656	    | otherwise  = Nothing
657	  where
658	    fm1 = simplifyGuardedOrReturn valuation fm0
659	    ppFm  = nest 2 . doubleQuotes . prettyGuarded
660	    ppMsg = nest 2 $ text "simplified formula:" $-$
661	                     nest 2 (vcat [ ppFm fm0, text "to", ppFm fm1])
662	
663	-- | Simplify a 'Guarded' formula by replacing atoms with their truth value,
664	-- if it can be determined. If nothing is simplified, returns the initial formula.
665	simplifyGuardedOrReturn :: (LNAtom -> Maybe Bool)
666	                        -- ^ Partial assignment for truth value of atoms.
667	                        -> LNGuarded
668	                        -- ^ Original formula
669	                        -> LNGuarded
670	                        -- ^ Simplified formula.
671	simplifyGuardedOrReturn valuation fm0 = {-trace (render ppMsg)-} fm1
672	  where
673	--     ppFm  = nest 2 . doubleQuotes . prettyGuarded
674	--     ppMsg = nest 2 $ text "simplified formula:" $-$
675	--                      nest 2 (vcat [ ppFm fm0, text "to", ppFm fm1])
676	
677	    fm1 = simp fm0
678	
679	    simp fm@(GAto ato)         = maybe fm gtf {-(trace (show (valuation =<< unbindAtom ato))-} (valuation =<< unbindAtom ato){-)-}
680	    simp (GDisj fms)           = gdisj $ map simp $ getDisj fms
681	    simp (GConj fms)           = gconj $ map simp $ getConj fms
682	    simp (GGuarded All [] atos gf)

471	prettyLFormula :: (HighlightDocument d, MonadFresh m, Ord c, Functor syn)
472	              => (ProtoAtom syn (VTerm c LVar) -> d)  -- ^ Function for pretty printing atoms
473	              -> ProtoLFormula syn c                  -- ^ Formula to pretty print.
474	              -> m d                             -- ^ Pretty printed formula.
475	prettyLFormula ppAtom =
476	    pp
477	  where
478	    extractFree (Free v)  = v
479	    extractFree (Bound i) = error $ "prettyFormula: illegal bound variable '" ++ show i ++ "'"
480	
481	    pp (Ato a)    = return $ ppAtom (fmap (mapLits (fmap extractFree)) a)
482	    pp (TF True)  = return $ operator_ "⊤"    -- "T"
483	    pp (TF False) = return $ operator_ "⊥"    -- "F"
484	
485	    pp (Not p)    = do
486	      p' <- pp p
487	      return $ operator_ "¬" <> opParens p' -- text "¬" <> parens (pp a)
488	      -- return $ operator_ "not" <> opParens p' -- text "¬" <> parens (pp a)
489	
490	    pp (Conn op p q) = do
491	        p' <- pp p
492	        q' <- pp q
493	        return $ sep [opParens p' <-> ppOp op, opParens q']
494	      where
495	        ppOp And = opLAnd
496	        ppOp Or  = opLOr
497	        ppOp Imp = opImp
498	        ppOp Iff = opIff
499	
500	    pp fm@(Qua _ _ _) =
501	        scopeFreshness $ do
502	            (vs,qua,fm') <- openFormulaPrefix fm
503	            d' <- pp fm'
504	            return $ sep
505	                     [ ppQuant qua <> ppVars vs <> operator_ "."
506	                     , nest 1 d']
507	      where
508	        ppVars       = fsep . map (text . show)
509	
510	        ppQuant All = opForall
511	        ppQuant Ex  = opExists

1	# Similarity audit — wellformedness cluster (`wf-clean`)
2	
3	Auditor role: clean-room similarity review. I read both the clean side and the
4	GPL Haskell originals (and, as a secondary leak check, the replaced Rust module
5	`crates/tamarin-parser/src/wf.rs`). Method: abstraction–filtration–comparison.
6	
7	- CLEAN SIDE: `workspace/wf-clean/src/{pretty,report,checks,formula,lib}.rs`
8	  (`ast.rs` is the provided interface header — not audited).
9	- HASKELL: `tamarin-prover/lib/theory/src/Theory/Tools/Wellformedness.hs`
10	  (+ its pretty-printing dependencies).
11	- INTERFACE GIVEN TO CLEAN ROOM: `interface/required_api.md`,
12	  `interface/ast_types.rs`, `SPEC.md`.
13	- CLEAN-ROOM EVIDENCE OF DERIVATION: `workspace/QUERIES.log` (per-probe oracle
14	  interaction log), `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md` (inferred behavioral spec),
15	  `oracle/captures/*.out` (37 pre-captured oracle outputs).
16	
17	## Verdict
18	
19	**PASS. No violations survive filtration.** Every clean/Haskell (or
20	clean/wf.rs) similarity resolves to one of the three filtered categories:
21	(a) a name or signature explicitly listed in `required_api.md`; (b) an exact
22	message string present in observed oracle output (captures) or logged as a live
23	oracle observation in `QUERIES.log`/`BEHAVIOR.md`; or (c) structure forced by
24	the `ast_types.rs` data model or by the block/underline output format.
25	
26	The clean code additionally *diverges* from `wf.rs` at every point the interface
27	does not constrain (see §6), which is positive evidence of independent
28	derivation rather than reproduction. One **process caveat** (not a clean-side
29	authorship violation, no redo) is recorded in §7: the interface header transmits
30	a port-specific function decomposition to the clean room.
31	
32	---
33	
34	## 1. report.rs
35	
36	- `WfError { topic: String, message: String }`, `impl WfError::new(topic: impl
37	  Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>)`, and `pub type WfReport =
38	  Vec<WfError>` (report.rs:7-22) are **byte-for-byte the surface in
39	  `required_api.md:4-6`**. Haskell models this as `type WfError = (Topic, Doc)` /
40	  `type WfErrorReport = [WfError]` (Wellformedness.hs:112-114). The struct field
41	  name `message` and the shortened `WfReport` come from the interface header, not
42	  from Haskell (which has an unnamed `Doc` and `WfErrorReport`).
43	  → **Filtered: API names given in interface docs.**
44	- `underline_topic` (report.rs:29-33) renders `title + "\n" + "="*n`. Haskell
45	  `underlineTopic` (Wellformedness.hs:168-171) and `wf.rs:400-408` BOTH append a
46	  trailing `"\n"`; the clean version does **not** — it assembles the blank line
47	  between header and body in `render_block`/`render_report` instead. Divergence
48	  from both prior implementations; behavior (the `=` underline) is observable.
49	  → **Filtered (observed); note the divergence as independence evidence.**
50	- `SUCCESS_LINE`, the `/* WARNING: ... */` wrapper, and the block layout
51	  (`render_report`, report.rs:41-57) reproduce observed oracle output verbatim
52	  (BEHAVIOR.md §"Overall report shape", captures). → **Filtered: observed output.**
53	- `insert_wf_before` (report.rs:61-76) has the exact signature from
54	  `required_api.md:23`. Its body (`position(anchor)` → `split_off` → splice) is
55	  the only reasonable implementation of the documented contract and differs in
56	  expression from `wf.rs:115-126` (clean uses `match`, wf.rs uses
57	  `unwrap_or(len)`). Haskell has no analogue (it builds the report by a fixed
58	  `concatMap`). → **Filtered: API-given signature; body forced by contract.**
59	
60	## 2. lib.rs
61	
62	- `check_theory` (lib.rs:36-56) runs the checks in the **observed** canonical
63	  topic order (BEHAVIOR.md §"Canonical topic order", ks1/ks3 probes) and then
64	  splices `public_names_report` via `insert_wf_before(after_public_names_topics())`.
65	  This run-then-splice shape matches `wf.rs:146-195` and is *not* Haskell's shape
66	  (Haskell simply lists `publicNamesReport` 4th in a `concatMap`,
67	  Wellformedness.hs:1271-1283). The shape is transmitted by the interface, which
68	  exposes `insert_wf_before` and `after_public_names_topics` as required API.
69	  → **Filtered: API-given.** (See §7 process caveat.)
70	- `after_public_names_topics` (lib.rs:19-33) lists topic strings — all observable
71	  output headers — in observed order. The clean list contains **only** the
72	  AST-derivable topics and omits wf.rs's `"Fact capitalization issues"`,
73	  `"Lemma annotations"`, `"Multiplication restriction of rules"`,
74	  `"Message Derivation Checks"` (wf.rs `WF_TOPIC_ORDER`, wf.rs:83-100). Divergence
75	  from wf.rs. → **Filtered: API-given function; content is observed topic order.**
76	- `check_if_lemmas_in_theory` (lib.rs:62-74) — API-given signature
77	  (required_api.md:19). The clean body is a plain set-difference with a
78	  self-documented "message text is a gap" (it does not reproduce Haskell's
79	  `checkIfLemmasInTheory` wording, Wellformedness.hs:1156-1171). → **Filtered.**
80	
81	## 3. checks.rs
82	
83	Public check function names are all derived from the **topic strings**
84	(observable output): `unbound_variables`, `mismatching_sorts`, `reserved_names`,
85	`fr_facts`, `special_facts`, `fact_arity`, `fact_multiplicity`, `formula_terms`,
86	`formula_guardedness`, `nat_sorts`, `subterm_convergence`. None match Haskell's
87	internal names (`unboundCheck`, `sortsClashCheck`, `reservedFactNameRules'`,
88	`freshFactArguments'`, `specialFactsUsage'`, `factUsage`, `checkTerms`,
89	`checkGuarded`, `natWellSortedReport`, `checkEquationsSubtermConvergence`) and
90	none match wf.rs's internal names (`unbound_report`, `variable_sort_clashes`,
91	`reserved_fact_name_rules`, `fresh_fact_arguments`, `special_facts_usage`,
92	`fact_usage`, `nat_well_sorted_report`, `subterm_convergence_report`).
93	→ **Filtered: names derived from observable topics.**
94	
95	Internal helpers (`collect_term_vars`, `collect_fact_vars`, `collect_pub_lits`,
96	`var_key`, `sort_rank`, `dedup_vars`, `variant_repr`, `gather_fact_uses`,
97	`render_fact_blocks`, `nearest_rhs`, `edit_distance`, `free_vars_formula`,
98	`atom_vars`, `guard_vars`, `find_unguarded`, `collect_nat_issues`, `is_subterm`,
99	`FactUse`, `FactId`, `protocol_rules`, `is_reserved`/`is_special`/
100	`is_builtin_factname`) — none share a name with a Haskell internal. Per-check
101	notes:
102	
103	- **Unbound variables** (checks.rs:132-176): message text is observed
104	  (BEHAVIOR.md §Unbound; probes p01/ub_pub/z5). The bound/used partition
105	  (bound = premises ∪ let-bound; used = actions ∪ conclusions ∪ let-RHS) and the
106	  `var_needs_binding` set {Fresh, Msg, Untagged, Nat} **diverge** from Haskell
107	  `unboundCheck` (Wellformedness.hs:493-511), which excludes only `LSortPub` and
108	  the special `NOW` node and has no explicit let handling (it relies on already-
109	  substituted facts + `originatesFromLookup`). Clean excludes `Node` entirely and
110	  handles `let` explicitly. → **Filtered: not Haskell's expression; observable.**
111	- **Mismatching sorts** (checks.rs:181-241): header text observed (p20/ms2,
112	  BEHAVIOR.md:86). Clean groups by `to_lowercase(name)` with variant =
113	  sort-prefix+name (index ignored). Haskell `sortsClashCheck`/`clashesOn`
114	  (Wellformedness.hs:154-161, 258-272) groups by `(lowerCase name, idx)` with
115	  variant = full `LVar`. Different normalization keys. → **Filtered.**
116	- **Reserved names** (checks.rs:246-277): position-dependent reserved sets
117	  ({K,KU,KD} on L/R; +{In,Out,Fr} in the middle) and the phrase strings are
118	  explicitly observed (QUERIES.log z9/z11/z12; BEHAVIOR.md §Reserved names).
119	  → **Filtered: observed output + behavior.**
120	- **Fr facts / Special facts** (checks.rs:282-348): trigger conditions and the
121	  `on left-hand-side:` / `on right-hand-side:` / lowercase-`rule` wording are
122	  observed (p14/p15/sf_pos/z12; BEHAVIOR.md). → **Filtered.**
123	- **Fact arity / multiplicity** (checks.rs:399-471): intro strings and the
124	  numbered block layout are observed (p02/p03/f_arity3; BEHAVIOR.md:112-121).
125	  Clean shares one `gather_fact_uses` + `render_fact_blocks` across both checks;
126	  Haskell shares one `theoryFacts`/`allClashes` across cap/arity/mult
127	  (Wellformedness.hs:636-689). This shared-grouping shape is the natural
128	  consequence of both checks keying on per-fact-name grouping (behavior), and the
129	  clean side factors the two *formatters* into one shared helper whereas Haskell
130	  keeps `formatArityIssue`/`formatMultipIssue` separate. → **Filtered: behavior-
131	  driven DRY; decomposition differs from Haskell.**
132	- **fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs** (checks.rs:500-582): API-given name
133	  (required_api.md:20). Identity triple `(name, arity, persistent)` is literally
134	  printed in the output line (`factName \`X' arity: N multiplicity: Linear`), so
135	  it is observation-derived, not lifted from Haskell's `factInfo`
136	  (Wellformedness.hs:174-175). The `edit_distance ≤ 3` suggestion threshold and
137	  "nearest RHS by name distance" behavior are observed (th2/thN; BEHAVIOR.md:128).
138	  `edit_distance` is textbook two-row Levenshtein; the name is generic. Clean's
139	  index numbering does **not** left-pad to width (unlike wf.rs `numbered_index_
140	  width`, wf.rs:1353/431), a divergence. → **Filtered.**
141	- **Public names** (checks.rs:588-654): API-given `public_names_report` /
142	  `public_names_report_from_pairs` (required_api.md:21-22). Header text observed
143	  (p32/p38; BEHAVIOR.md:74-81). Clean's pair order is **`(name, rule)`** — the
144	  *opposite* of wf.rs's **`(rule, name)`** (wf.rs:1455) — and clean groups by
145	  first-seen order, whereas both Haskell (`clashesOn` → `sortOn`) and wf.rs sort
146	  by lowercased name. Strong divergence. → **Filtered; independence evidence.**
147	- **Formula terms** (checks.rs:657-755): the `` `Free x' `` rendering was
148	  observed live (QUERIES.log p05 `-> "Formula terms" (\`Free y')`), and the help
149	  text appears in `oracle/captures/…Typing_and_Destructors…out`. Clean tracks a
150	  binder stack (`free_vars_formula` push/pop on quantifiers) because
151	  `ast_types.rs` represents quantified vars by *name* (`Forall(Vec<VarSpec>,…)`),
152	  not de Bruijn — so the stack is forced by the data model. Haskell `checkTerms`
153	  (Wellformedness.hs:960-985) needs no stack because its LNFormula already carries
154	  `Bound`/`Free`. `atom_vars` (checks.rs:696-712) maps each `Atom` variant to its
155	  constituent terms — forced by the `Atom` enum in `ast_types.rs:311-320` — and
156	  even diverges from Haskell `atomTerms` (Wellformedness.hs:908-915) by including
157	  predicate-atom facts (Haskell returns `[]` for `Syntactic`). → **Filtered.**
158	- **Formula guardedness** (checks.rs:761-836): the message body
159	  ("cannot be converted to a guarded formula" / "unguarded variable(s) … in the
160	  subformula … in the formula") is documented as observed (QUERIES.log
161	  p21/p39; BEHAVIOR.md:140-145). The detection (`find_unguarded`/`guard_vars`:
162	  a quantifier is "guarded" if its bound vars all appear under some Action/Pred
163	  atom) is a self-declared partial heuristic and is nothing like Haskell's real
164	  `formulaToGuarded` conversion. → **Filtered: observed output; own algorithm.**
165	- **Nat Sorts** (checks.rs:842-888): message observed (t_nat; BEHAVIOR.md:147).
166	  Clean collects offending *vars* (sort ≠ Nat) under a `%+`; Haskell
167	  `nonWellSorted` (Wellformedness.hs:293-304) collects offending *terms* recursing
168	  through nested nat-plus. Different traversal. → **Filtered.**
169	- **Subterm convergence** (checks.rs:894-930): intro + manual-URL string appears
170	  in captures (OIDC/POIDC/eccDAA). `is_subterm` is a self-declared structural
171	  approximation, unrelated to Haskell's `filterNonSubtermCtxtRule`. → **Filtered.**
172	
173	## 4. pretty.rs
174	
175	Every rule traces to an observed probe (t_terms/t_xor/t_nat/f_nullary;
176	BEHAVIOR.md §"Term pretty-printer"). Sort sigils `~ $ % #`, pair flattening
177	`<…>`, `f(a, b)`, `'lit'`, `^` without parens, AC operators parenthesized,
178	`%1`, and `DH_neutral` (present in `oracle/captures/…RYY_PFS…out`) are all
179	observed output. Independent-derivation signals vs. wf.rs:
180	
181	- `Term::NumberOne => "1"` (pretty.rs:87) vs. wf.rs `NumberOne => "one"`
182	  (wf.rs:466). **Opposite rendering.**
183	- Only `Xor` operands are sorted, by **rendered-string** sort (pretty.rs:71-74);
184	  wf.rs sorts **all** AC operators (Mult/Union/Xor/NatPlus) by a term `Ord`
185	  (`cmp_wf_term`, wf.rs:517-521). Different scope and different comparator.
186	
187	→ **Filtered: observed output; clear divergence from wf.rs.**
188	
189	## 5. formula.rs
190	
191	Single-line unicode printer `pp_formula` (formula.rs:10-42). Operator glyphs
192	(⊥ ⊤ ¬ ∧ ∨ ⇒ ⇔ ∀ ∃ = < ⋖ ⊏ @ last(…)) are formula-rendering output; the
193	module header states it was "calibrated from the oracle (e.g. ks1 lemma L2)".
194	Clean **fully parenthesizes** every binary connective (`({}) ∧ ({})`), unlike
195	Haskell's precedence-minimized `prettyLNFormula`. → **Filtered: observed/
196	calibrated; own parenthesization.** (`⋖` LessMset / `⊏` Subterm may be rarer in
197	the probed lemmas, but both are standard notation and the module is declared a
198	documented partial; not worth a redo.)
199	
200	## 6. Secondary check — divergence from replaced `wf.rs`
201	
202	Instruction: flag only where clean matches `wf.rs` structure in ways Haskell and
203	behavior do not explain. **Result: no such match.** Every clean/wf.rs match is
204	covered by `required_api.md` (WfError, new, WfReport, insert_wf_before,
205	after_public_names_topics, public_names_report[_from_pairs], topics,
206	underline_topic, render_report, check_theory, check_if_lemmas_in_theory,
207	fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs). The clean code diverges from wf.rs everywhere the API is
208	silent:
209	
210	| Aspect | clean | wf.rs |
211	|---|---|---|
212	| `_from_pairs` tuple order | `(name, rule)` | `(rule, name)` |
213	| public-names grouping | first-seen order | sorted (`clashesOn`) |
214	| `NumberOne` render | `"1"` | `"one"` |
215	| AC operand sort | Xor only, string sort | all AC, term `Ord` |
216	| `underline_topic` trailing `\n` | none | present |
217	| numbered index padding | none | width-padded |
218	| internal sort order (`sort_rank`) | Msg<Pub<Fresh<Node<Nat | Pub<Fresh<Msg<Node<Nat |
219	| check fn names | topic-derived | port-derived |
220	
221	No Haskell-internal identifiers or `.hs:` source citations appear anywhere in the
222	clean sources (grep confirmed empty).
223	
224	## 7. Process caveat (NOT a clean-side violation; no redo)
225	
226	`required_api.md` hands the clean room three functions whose *existence* is a
227	port-specific implementation seam with **no behavioral justification derivable
228	from the oracle**: `insert_wf_before`, `after_public_names_topics`, and the
229	`public_names_report` / `public_names_report_from_pairs` split. The oracle never
230	exposes these seams; the clean room implemented them only because the interface
231	required them. Per the protocol these are "API names given in the provided
232	interface docs" and are explicitly *not* clean-room violations, so no redo is
233	issued to `wf-clean`. This is flagged upward to the process owners: the
234	interoperability header for future clusters should prefer conveying *behavior/
235	ordering* over port-internal function decompositions, to keep the clean room from
236	inheriting the port's architecture. The clean implementations of these functions
237	are, in any case, independently expressed (see §1, §2, §3).
238	
239	---
240	
241	## Round 2 incremental audit
242	
243	Scope: only the round-2 additions — six new checks (`fresh_public_constants`,
244	`reserved_prefixes`, `diff_left_right` → "Left rule"/"Right rule",
245	`lemma_annotations`, `multiplication_restriction`), the rule pretty-printer
246	(`pp_rule`/`pp_fact_list`), the `fillSep`-style wrapper (`fill_words`, width 69),
247	`indent_block`, the diff-mode term/fact projection
248	(`project_term`/`project_fact`/`project_facts`/`rule_matches_projection`), and
249	the `lib.rs` ordering changes. Method unchanged: abstraction–filtration–
250	comparison against `Wellformedness.hs`, `Model/Rule.hs`, `Model/Fact.hs`,
251	`Term/Term.hs`, and `Text/Pretty.hs`.
252	
253	**Round-2 verdict: PASS. No violations survive filtration (0 redo).** Every
254	round-2 string, layout, indent, width, ordering, and trigger is corroborated as
255	observed oracle output by a named probe in `QUERIES.log` and `BEHAVIOR.md`, and
256	by the byte-parity captures in `wf-clean/tests/fixtures/r2_*.txt` (each carries
257	the oracle's `WARNING …`/`*/` framing). The exact strings are therefore
258	compatibility content per PROTOCOL §"Forbidden … Exact output strings … MUST be
259	taken from observed oracle output". Where the round-2 code touches an algorithm,
260	it is either forced by the observed output and the `ast.rs` data model, or a
261	materially different (usually simpler / gapped) implementation than the Haskell —
262	divergences are recorded below as independence evidence.
263	
264	### R2-1. Fresh public constants
265	- Clean `fresh_public_constants` (checks.rs:1038-1063), `collect_fresh_lits`
266	  (checks.rs:117-132) vs Haskell `freshNamesReport'`
267	  (Wellformedness.hs:444-452).

271	  via a generic `universeBi` traversal; the clean side walks the three fact lists
272	  explicitly and renders each literal through its own term printer (`~'foo'`).
273	  Different traversal, own name (`fresh_public_constants` is topic-derived; the
274	  Haskell internal is `freshNamesReport`). → **Filtered: observed output; not
275	  Haskell's expression. No redo.**
276	
277	### R2-2. Reserved prefixes (diff mode only)
278	- Clean `reserved_prefixes` (checks.rs:1070-1111), `reserved_prefix_header_words`
279	  (checks.rs:1113-1131), `RESERVED_PREFIXES` (checks.rs:31) vs Haskell

285	  Haskell `show` of a tuple, but it is emitted by the oracle, so it is

289	  whereas Haskell lowercases first (`take 8 (map toLower name) == "diffintr" …`);
290	  independence evidence (and a latent behavioral difference, not a similarity).
291	  → **Filtered: observed output. No redo.**
292	
293	### R2-3. `fill_words` — fillSep-style wrapper at width 69
294	- Clean `fill_words` (checks.rs:169-193), `FILL_WIDTH = 69` (checks.rs:36) vs

316	  `\n  \n` blank spacing) and the LEFT-before-RIGHT + "only the left is reported
317	  when both differ" behavior are observed (probes diff_left_right_mismatch,
318	  diff_right_rule_mismatch, diff_both; BEHAVIOR.md:174-184; fixtures
319	  r2_left_rule/r2_right_rule).
320	- Diff projection (the explicit round-2 focus): both replace `diff(a,b)` with `a`
321	  (left) / `b` (right) recursively, structurally preserving everything else — the
322	  only reasonable realization of the observed projection. But the clean
323	  decomposition is driven by the `ast.rs` `Term` enum (dedicated `Term::Diff`
324	  arm plus `App`/`AlgApp`/`Pair`/`BinOp`/`PatMatch` arms), not by Haskell's
325	  uniform `FAPP sym ts` with an `o == diffSym` test; the clean side parameterizes
326	  on a plain `bool left` where Haskell uses a 4-way `DiffType`
327	  (DiffLeft/Right/Both/None); and consistency is decided by comparing
328	  pretty-printed fact lists (`facts_pp`) rather than Haskell's structural
329	  `equalUpToAddedActions` on a reconstructed rule. Names diverge entirely
330	  (`project_*` vs `getLeft*`/`getSide`). → **Filtered: forced by observed
331	  behavior + data model; materially different expression. No redo.**
332	
333	### R2-5. Lemma annotations
334	- Clean `lemma_annotations` (checks.rs:1227-1245) vs Haskell
335	  `lemmaAttributeReport` (Wellformedness.hs:924-932).

337	  (`reuse` attribute AND `exists-trace`, not reuse alone) are observed (probes
338	  exists_trace_reuse/la_alltraces/la_multi; BEHAVIOR.md:186-190). The clean
339	  function name is topic-derived ("Lemma annotations") and differs from the
340	  Haskell internal `lemmaAttributeReport`. → **Filtered: observed output +
341	  behavior. No redo.**
342	
343	### R2-6. Multiplication restriction of rules
344	- Clean `multiplication_restriction` (checks.rs:1254-1278), `collect_mult_terms`
345	  (checks.rs:137-155) vs Haskell `multRestrictedReport'`
346	  (Wellformedness.hs:1047-1099), `multTerms` (Wellformedness.hs:1094-1096).
347	- The three intro strings, the two indent-4 rule blocks, and
348	  "Terms with multiplication:  <terms>" (two spaces) are observed (probes
349	  mul_multi/mul_act/mul_pc/mul_terms; BEHAVIOR.md:192-206; fixture
350	  r2_multiplication). The clean check is materially simpler than Haskell's:
351	  Haskell abstracts reducible lhs symbols to fresh vars (`abstractRule`) and also
352	  reports "Variables that occur only in rhs:"; the clean side prints the SAME
353	  rule twice and omits the vars-only-in-rhs branch (both documented gaps,
354	  BEHAVIOR.md:201-204) — strong independence evidence. `collect_mult_terms`
355	  collects maximal `*`-subterms (stop at a product, else recurse) exactly as the
356	  observed output requires; the recursion arms follow the `ast.rs` `Term`
357	  variants, not Haskell's `FApp (AC Mult)` view. → **Filtered: observed output;
358	  own (gapped) algorithm; behavior-forced maximal-product collection. No redo.**
359	
360	### R2-7. Rule pretty-printer (`pp_rule` / `pp_fact_list`)
361	- Clean `pp_rule` (pretty.rs:147-158), `pp_fact_list` (pretty.rs:129-136) vs
362	  Haskell `prettyNamedRule` (Model/Rule.hs:1280-1292), `prettyRuleRestrGen`
363	  (Model/Rule.hs:1253-1269), `prettyRule` (Model/Rule.hs:1276-1277).
364	- The whole layout — "rule (modulo E) NAME:", 3-space indent, `[ … ]` /`[ ]`
365	  fact lists, `-->` vs `--[ … ]->` — is observed (BEHAVIOR.md:244-254; fixtures).
366	  `pp_fact_list` mirrors Haskell's `ppFactsList` only in that both emit `[ … ]`,
367	  a trivial output-driven helper with a different name. The clean printer emits a
368	  single-line body and documents that the oracle wraps long rules (gap,
369	  BEHAVIOR.md:253-254) — Haskell's `sep`/`fsep` wraps; divergence. → **Filtered:
370	  observed output; trivial DRY; own single-line form. No redo.**

373	- Clean `indent_block` (checks.rs:158-164) vs Haskell `nest`
374	  (used at Wellformedness.hs:403-411, 1056-1064). Generic "prefix every line with
375	  N spaces" utility; behaviorally the block-indent the observed output shows, with

385	  comb1/diff_mega/diff_place). Notably the clean side lists the fact-family and

177	  hcat  = Doc . P.hcat . map getPrettyLibraryDoc
178	  hsep  = Doc . P.hsep . map getPrettyLibraryDoc
179	  ($$) (Doc d1) (Doc d2) = Doc $ (P.$$) d1 d2
180	  ($-$) (Doc d1) (Doc d2) = Doc $ (P.$+$) d1 d2
181	  vcat  = Doc . P.vcat . map getPrettyLibraryDoc
182	  sep   = Doc . P.sep . map getPrettyLibraryDoc
183	  cat   = Doc . P.cat . map getPrettyLibraryDoc
184	  fsep  = Doc . P.fsep . map getPrettyLibraryDoc
185	  fcat  = Doc . P.fcat . map getPrettyLibraryDoc
186	  nest i (Doc d) = Doc $ P.nest i d
187	  caseEmptyDoc yes no (Doc d) = if P.isEmpty d then yes else no
188	
189	instance Semigroup Doc where
190	    Doc d1 <> Doc d2 = Doc $ (P.<>) d1 d2
191	
192	instance Monoid Doc where
193	    mempty = Doc $ P.empty
194	
195	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
196	-- Additional combinators
197	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
198	
199	-- | Nest a document surrounded by a leading and a finishing document breaking
200	-- lead, body, and finish onto separate lines, if they don't fit on a single
201	-- line.
202	nestBetween :: Document d =>
203	               Int -- ^ Indent of body
204	            -> d -- ^ Leading document
205	            -> d -- ^ Finishing document
206	            -> d -- ^ Body document
207	            -> d
208	nestBetween n l r x = sep [l, nest n x, r]
209	
210	-- | Nest a document surrounded by a leading and a finishing document with an
211	-- non-compulsory break between lead and body.
212	nestShort :: Document d =>
213	             Int -- ^ Indent of body
214	          -> d -- ^ Leading document
215	          -> d -- ^ Finishing document
216	          -> d -- ^ Body document
217	          -> d
218	nestShort n lead finish body = sep [lead $$ nest n body, finish]
219	
220	-- | Nest document between two strings and indent body by @length lead + 1@.
221	nestShort' :: Document d => String -> String -> d -> d
222	nestShort' lead finish =
223	  nestShort (length lead + 1) (text lead) (text finish)
224	
225	-- | Like 'nestShort' but doesn't print the lead and finish, if the document is
226	-- empty.
227	nestShortNonEmpty :: Document d => Int -> d -> d -> d -> d
228	nestShortNonEmpty n lead finish body =
229	  caseEmptyDoc emptyDoc (nestShort n lead finish body) body
230	
231	-- | Like 'nestShort'' but doesn't print the lead and finish, if the document is
232	-- empty.
233	nestShortNonEmpty' :: Document d => String -> String -> d -> d
234	nestShortNonEmpty' lead finish =
235	  nestShortNonEmpty (length lead + 1) (text lead) (text finish)
236	
237	-- | Output text with a fixed width: if it is smaller then nothing happens,
238	-- otherwise care is taken to make the text appear having the given width.
239	fixedWidthText :: Document d => Int -> String -> d
240	fixedWidthText n cs
241	  | length cs <= n  = text cs
242	  | otherwise = text as <> zeroWidthText bs
243	  where
244	    (as,bs) = splitAt n cs
245	
246	-- | Print string as symbol having width 1.
247	symbol :: Document d => String -> d
248	symbol = fixedWidthText 1
249	
250	-- | Number a list of documents that are vertically separated by the given
251	-- separator.
252	numbered :: Document d => d -> [d] -> d
253	numbered _   [] = emptyDoc
254	numbered vsep ds =
255	    foldr1 ($-$) $ intersperse vsep $ map pp $ zip [(1::Int)..] ds
256	  where
257	    n         = length ds
258	    nWidth    = length (show n)
259	    pp (i, d) = text (flushRight nWidth (show i)) <> d
260	
261	-- | Number a list of documents with numbers terminated by "." and vertically
262	-- separate using an empty line.
263	numbered' :: Document d => [d] -> d
264	numbered' = numbered (text "") . map (text ". " <>)
